name: kong
explanation: Modern Kong exposes Prometheus/OpenMetrics on the Admin API at `http://<host>:8001/metrics` (Prometheus plugin) — the recommended path documented in the README. The legacy `kong_status_url` mode hits `http://<host>:8001/status/` on the same Admin API, also unauthenticated by default. Both endpoints share the same well-known port (8001) and don't require credentials in the default deployment. The check dispatches to `KongCheck` (OpenMetricsBaseCheckV2) when `openmetrics_endpoint` is set, otherwise to the legacy status parser. An OpenMetrics port scan on 8001 with `/metrics` is the canonical generic case.
notes: Production deployments that protect the Admin API with RBAC or a key-auth plugin would shift this toward `credentials-required`, but the upstream-recommended setup leaves the metrics endpoint open on the internal admin port. Closest to pattern A (OpenMetrics on a known port). No auto_conf.yaml ships today, but the spec assumes the canonical URL.
